claim: Hamel Husain writes hamel.dev as an independent ML consultant at Parlance Labs covering evals, error analysis, LLM-as-judge and shipping LLM products.
quote: Hamel Husain... independent consultant helping companies build AI products... operates as an ML engineer and independent consultant at Parlance Labs.

claim: Hamel Husain and Shreya Shankar co-teach the AI Evals for Engineers and PMs course, with 4,500+ professionals from 500+ companies enrolled and an August 2026 cohort with refreshed material.
quote: An August 2026 cohort's material is completely refreshed to cover advancements in the field. The course covers prioritization strategies, LLM judges, evaluation approaches, and analytical techniques for identifying AI failure modes.

claim: Eugene Yan blogs at eugeneyan.com on applied ML and LLMs (evals, RAG, fine-tuning, caching, guardrails, defensive UX) and is now Member of Technical Staff at Anthropic as of 2026.
quote: Eugene Yan is currently a Member of Technical Staff at Anthropic building recommendation systems and AI-powered products that serve customers at scale. He started this role in 2026.

claim: Applied LLMs (applied-llms.org) is a joint publication by Eugene Yan, Bryan Bischof, Charles Frye, Hamel Husain, Jason Liu and Shreya Shankar focused on 'how to apply LLMs effectively' from the year-of-building-with-LLMs cohort.
quote: The publication concentrates on 'how to apply LLMs effectively.' Its content addresses practical aspects of implementing machine learning and AI systems in real-world contexts.

claim: Latent Space (swyx + Alessio Fanelli) is the AI Engineer newsletter and podcast, started in 2022/2023 and reaching over 10 million readers/listeners in 2025.
quote: Latent Space is an AI Engineer newsletter and top technical AI podcast. The newsletter was first started by swyx in 2022, and the podcast was then started in 2023, cohosted by Alessio and swyx.

claim: Latent Space's 2026 plan adds AINews daily commentary, more YouTube/video formats, and a new AI for Science show — becoming a podcast network.
quote: The game plan for 2026's Latent Space is threefold: More shows and more hosts... AINews has joined Latent Space to give a place for regular daily updates and commentary. Latent Space is becoming a podcast network.

claim: Jason Liu (jxnl.co) is the creator of Instructor (6M+ monthly downloads) and writes/consults on RAG, agents and structured outputs.
quote: Jason Liu is the creator of Instructor, a Python library for structured LLM outputs that OpenAI cited as inspiration for their structured output feature. Instructor has 6M+ monthly downloads.

claim: Jason Liu runs 'Systematically Improving RAG Applications' distilling consulting experience scaling RAG in production for engineers from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and others.
quote: Jason Liu distills years of consulting experience helping companies build, improve, and scale RAG systems in production... students from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Microsoft, Amazon, McKinsey, and others.

claim: Shreya Shankar (sh-reya.com) is a Berkeley researcher whose Evals for AI Engineers book ships Spring 2026 with O'Reilly and whose research underpins LLM-as-judge methodology.
quote: Shreya Shankar has a book being published by O'Reilly in Spring 2026, which focuses on AI evaluations... methods for error analysis of LLM agents and for trustworthy, scalable LLM-as-judge evaluation.

claim: Chip Huyen blogs at huyenchip.com on AI engineering and ML systems in production, with her 2025 'AI Engineering' book widely treated as foundational reading for 2026 AI engineers.
quote: Chip Huyen is a writer and computer scientist whose focus is on ML/AI systems in production. She previously worked on ML tooling at NVIDIA (core dev of NeMo), Snorkel AI, and Netflix.

claim: Phillip Carter at Honeycomb writes the canonical posts on observability-driven development for LLMs and authored O'Reilly's Observability for Large Language Models.
quote: the only way to write good software at scale is by looping in production via observability—not by test-driven development, but observability-driven development, meaning shipping sooner, observing results.

claim: Charles Frye (Modal) produces practitioner-facing material on GPUs and AI infra, including 'What every AI engineer needs to know about GPUs'.
quote: Charles Frye is an AI Engineer at Modal Labs with expertise in Python, deep learning, and statistics... He has taught thousands the entire stack of AI application development through work at Weights and Biases, Full Stack Deep Learning, and Modal.

claim: Bryan Bischof (Head of AI at Hex, leading the Magic copilot) is a co-author of the influential 'What We Learned from a Year of Building with LLMs' series, focusing on production AI engineering.
quote: Bryan Bischof is the Head of AI at Hex, where he leads the team of engineers building Magic—the data science and analytics copilot.

claim: Hugo Bowne-Anderson's Vanishing Gradients podcast and Substack newsletter cover agents, evals, multimodal systems and data infra for AI builders, with active 2026 series on next-level AI evals.
quote: Vanishing Gradients is a podcast for people who build with AI, featuring long-format conversations with people shaping the field about agents, evals, multimodal systems, data infrastructure, and the tools behind them.

claim: Dan Becker co-teaches Mastering LLMs for Developers & Data Scientists with Hamel Husain on Maven; the 40+ hour course is organized by evals, RAG, fine-tuning, app building and prompts.
quote: Dan has worked in AI since 2011... contributed code to TensorFlow as a data scientist at Google and he has taught online deep learning courses to over 250k people.

claim: Simon Willison's weekly Substack and simonwillison.net are leading high-cadence sources for hands-on LLM engineering commentary, code-with-LLMs workflows, and yearly 'things we learned about LLMs' roundups.
quote: Simon Willison publishes a weekly email newsletter (newsletter 17 as of April 24, 2026)... covers AI, LLMs, web engineering, open source, data science, Datasette, SQLite, Python and more.
